L. 116–260, § 1002(g)(2)(B)“(1) Not later than , each agency shall, to the maximum extent practicable, install in Federal buildings owned by the United States all energy and water conservation measures with payback periods of less than 10 years, as determined by using the methods and procedures developed pursuant to .January 1, 2005section 8254 of this title“(2) The Secretary may waive the requirements of this subsection for any agency for such periods as the Secretary may determine if the Secretary finds that the agency is taking all practicable steps to meet the requirements and that the requirements of this subsection will pose an unacceptable burden upon the agency. (1) which read as follows: “An agency may exclude, from the energy consumption requirements for the year 2000 established under subsection (a) and the requirements of subsection (b)(1), any Federal building or collection of Federal buildings, and the associated energy consumption and gross square footage, if the head of such agency finds that compliance with such requirements would be impractical. A finding of impracticability shall be based on the energy intensiveness of activities carried out in such Federal buildings or collection of Federal buildings, the type and amount of energy consumed, the technical feasibility of making the desired changes, and, in the cases of the Departments of Defense and Energy, the unique character of certain facilities operated by such Departments.”Pub. Survey of Energy Saving Potential, , , which authorized Secretary of Energy to carry out an energy survey to determine maximum potential cost effective energy savings in federally used buildings and recommend cost effective energy efficiency and renewable energy improvements in those buildings, devise a plan for implementing such survey, and report its findings and conclusions to Congress, was repealed by , , .Pub. children.heading: Energy performance requirement for Federal buildings
children.label: a Energy performance requirement for Federal buildings

children.text: Subject to paragraph (2), each agency shall apply energy conservation measures to, and shall improve the design for the construction of, the Federal buildings of the agency (including each industrial or laboratory facility) so that the energy consumption per gross square foot of the Federal buildings of the agency in fiscal years 2006 through 2015 is reduced, as compared with the energy consumption per gross square foot of the Federal buildings of the agency in fiscal year 2003, by the percentage specified in the following table:Fiscal YearPercentage Reduction200622007420089200912201015201118201221201324201427201530.

An agency may exclude from the requirements of paragraph (1) any building, and the associated energy consumption and gross square footage, in which energy intensive activities are carried out. Each agency shall identify and list in each report made under the buildings designated by it for such exclusion.section 8258(a) of this title

Not later than , the Secretary shall review the results of the implementation of the energy performance requirement established under paragraph (1) and submit to Congress recommendations concerning energy performance requirements for fiscal years 2016 through 2025.December 31, 2014

children.heading: Energy and water management requirement for Federal agencies
children.label: b Energy and water management requirement for Federal agencies
A
children.text: not later than , to the maximum extent practicable, begin installing in Federal buildings owned by the United States all energy and water conservation measures determined by the Secretary to be life cycle cost-effective (as defined in subsection (f)(1)); andOctober 1, 2022

children.label: B
children.text: complete the installation described in subparagraph (A) as soon as practicable after the date referred to in that subparagraph.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: If an agency fails to comply with paragraph (1), the agency shall submit to the Secretary, using guidelines developed by the Secretary, an explanation of the reasons for the failure.

children.heading: .—Report to Congress
children.label: B .—Report to Congress
children.text: Not later than , and every 2 years thereafter, the Secretary shall submit to Congress a report that describes any noncompliance by an agency with the requirements of paragraph (1).January 1, 2022
This subsection shall not apply to an agency’s facilities that generate or transmit electric energy or to the uranium enrichment facilities operated by the Department of Energy.
An agency may participate in the Environmental Protection Agency’s “Green Lights” program for purposes of receiving technical assistance in complying with the requirements of this section.

children.label: i
children.text: compliance with those requirements would be impracticable;

children.label: ii
children.text: the agency has completed and submitted all federally required energy or water management reports;

children.label: iii
children.text: the agency has achieved compliance with the energy or water efficiency requirements of this chapter, the Energy Policy Act of 1992, Executive orders, and other Federal law; and

children.label: iv
children.text: the agency has implemented all practicable, life cycle cost-effective projects with respect to the Federal building or collection of Federal buildings to be excluded.

children.label: i
children.text: the energy or water intensiveness of activities carried out in the Federal building or collection of Federal buildings; or

children.label: ii
children.text: the fact that the Federal building or collection of Federal buildings is used in the performance of a national security function.

Each agency shall identify and list, in each report made under , the Federal buildings designated by it for such exclusion. The Secretary shall review such findings for consistency with the standards for exclusion set forth in paragraph (1), and may within 90 days after receipt of the findings, reverse the exclusion. In the case of any such reversal, the agency shall comply with the requirements of subsections (a) and (b)(1) for the building concerned.section 8258(a) of this title
Not later than 180 days after , the Secretary shall issue guidelines that establish criteria for exclusions under paragraph (1).August 8, 2005

children.label: A
children.text: designate personnel primarily responsible for achieving such requirements;

children.label: B
children.text: identify high priority projects through calculation of payback periods;

children.label: C
children.text: take maximum advantage of contracts authorized under subchapter VII of this chapter, of financial incentives and other services provided by utilities for efficiency investment, and of other forms of financing to reduce the direct costs to the Government; and

children.label: D
children.text: otherwise implement this part;

perform energy and water surveys of its Federal buildings to the extent necessary and update such surveys as needed, incorporating any relevant information obtained from the survey conducted pursuant to ;section 8258b of this title
using such surveys, determine the cost and payback period of energy and water conservation measures likely to achieve the requirements of this section;
install energy and water conservation measures that will achieve the requirements of this section through the methods and procedures established pursuant to ; andsection 8254 of this title
ensure that the operation and maintenance procedures applied under this section are continued.

children.heading: Metering of energy and water use
children.label: e Metering of energy and water use

children.heading: Deadline
children.label: 1 Deadline
children.text: By , in accordance with guidelines established by the Secretary under paragraph (2), all Federal buildings shall, for the purposes of efficient use of energy and water and reduction in the cost of electricity and water used in such buildings, be metered. Each agency shall use, to the maximum extent practicable, advanced meters or advanced metering devices that provide data at least daily and that measure at least hourly consumption of electricity and water in the Federal buildings of the agency. Not later than , each agency shall provide for equivalent metering of natural gas and steam, in accordance with guidelines established by the Secretary under paragraph (2). Such data shall be incorporated into existing Federal energy and water tracking systems and made available to Federal facility managers.October 1, 2022October 1, 2016

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Not later than 180 days after , the Secretary, in consultation with the Department of Defense, the General Services Administration, representatives from the metering industry, utility industry, energy services industry, energy efficiency industry, energy efficiency advocacy organizations, national laboratories, universities, Federal facility managers, and any other person the Secretary deems necessary, shall establish guidelines for agencies to carry out paragraph (1).August 8, 2005

children.label: I
children.text: the cost of metering and the reduced cost of operation and maintenance expected to result from metering;

children.label: II
children.text: the extent to which metering is expected to result in increased potential for energy and water management, increased potential for energy and water savings and energy and water efficiency improvement, and cost and energy and water savings due to utility contract aggregation; and

children.label: III
children.text: the measurement and verification protocols of the Department of Energy;

children.label: ii
children.text: include recommendations concerning the amount of funds and the number of trained personnel necessary to gather and use the metering information to track and reduce energy and water use;

children.label: iii
children.text: establish priorities for types and locations of buildings to be metered based on cost-effectiveness and a schedule of one or more dates, not later than 1 year after the date of issuance of the guidelines, on which the requirements specified in paragraph (1) shall take effect; and

children.label: iv
children.text: establish exclusions from the requirements specified in paragraph (1) based on the de minimis quantity of energy and water use of a Federal building, industrial process, or structure.

children.heading: Update
children.label: C Update
children.text: Not later than 180 days after , the Secretary shall update the guidelines established under subparagraph (A) to take into account water efficiency requirements under this section.December 27, 2020

children.label: A
children.text: how the agency will designate personnel primarily responsible for achieving the requirements; and

children.label: B
children.text: a demonstration by the agency, complete with documentation, of any finding that advanced meters or advanced metering devices (as those terms are used in paragraph (1)), are not practicable.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Not later than 180 days after , the Secretary of Energy, in consultation with the Secretary of Defense and the Administrator of General Services, shall develop, and issue a report on, best practices for the use of advanced metering of energy and water use in Federal facilities, buildings, and equipment by Federal agencies.December 27, 2020

children.label: i
children.text: summaries and analysis of the reports by agencies under paragraph (3);

children.label: I
children.text: potential common communications standards to allow data sharing and reporting;

children.label: II
children.text: means of facilitating continuous commissioning of buildings and evidence-based maintenance of buildings and building systems; and

children.label: III
children.text: standards for sufficient levels of security and protection against cyber threats to ensure systems cannot be controlled by unauthorized persons; and

children.label: I
children.text: the types of advanced metering and monitoring systems being piloted, tested, or installed in Federal buildings; and

children.label: II
children.text: existing techniques used within the private sector or other non-Federal government buildings.

children.heading: Use of energy and water efficiency measures in Federal buildings
children.label: f Use of energy and water efficiency measures in Federal buildings
I
children.text: the design documentation and intent of the facility; and

children.label: II
children.text: the operational needs of the owner of the facility, including preparation of operation personnel; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the primary goal of which is to ensure fully functional systems that can be properly operated and maintained during the useful life of the facility.

children.label: I
children.text: ensuring compliance with this subsection by the facility; and

children.label: II
children.text: reducing energy use at the facility.

children.label: I
children.text: a contractor of a facility;

children.label: II
children.text: a part-time employee of a facility; and

children.label: III
children.text: an individual who is responsible for multiple facilities.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The term “facility” means any building, installation, structure, or other property (including any applicable fixtures) owned or operated by, or constructed or manufactured and leased to, the Federal Government.

children.label: I
children.text: a group of facilities at a single location or multiple locations managed as an integrated operation; and

children.label: II
children.text: contractor-operated facilities owned by the Federal Government.

children.heading: Exclusions
children.label: iii Exclusions
children.text: The term “facility” does not include any land or site for which the cost of utilities is not paid by the Federal Government.

children.heading: Life cycle cost-effective
children.label: D Life cycle cost-effective
children.text: The term “life cycle cost-effective”, with respect to a measure, means a measure, the estimated savings of which exceed the estimated costs over the lifespan of the measure, as determined in accordance with .section 8254 of this title

children.heading: Ongoing commissioning
children.label: E Ongoing commissioning
children.text: The term “ongoing commissioning” means an ongoing process of commissioning using monitored data, the primary goal of which is to ensure continuous optimum performance of a facility, in accordance with design or operating needs, over the useful life of the facility, while meeting facility occupancy requirements.

children.label: I
children.text: the estimated initial implementation cost of the measure (other than financing costs); by

children.label: aa
children.text: net savings in estimated energy and water costs; and

children.label: bb
children.text: operations, maintenance, repair, replacement, and other direct costs.

children.heading: Modifications and exceptions
children.label: ii Modifications and exceptions
children.text: The Secretary, in guidelines issued pursuant to paragraph (6), may make such modifications and provide such exceptions to the calculation of the payback period of a measure as the Secretary determines to be appropriate to achieve the purposes of this chapter.

children.label: i
children.text: of commissioning a facility or system beyond the project development and warranty phases of the facility or system; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the primary goal of which is to ensure optimum performance of a facility, in accordance with design or current operating needs, over the useful life of the facility, while meeting building occupancy requirements.

children.heading: Retrocommissioning
children.label: H Retrocommissioning
children.text: The term “retrocommissioning” means a process of commissioning a facility or system that was not commissioned at the time of construction of the facility or system.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Each Federal agency shall designate an energy manager responsible for implementing this subsection and reducing energy and water use at each facility that meets criteria under subparagraph (B).

children.heading: Covered facilities
children.label: B Covered facilities
children.text: The Secretary shall develop criteria, after consultation with affected agencies, efficiency advocates, and energy and utility service providers, that cover, at a minimum, Federal facilities, including central utility plants and distribution systems and other energy intensive operations, that constitute at least 75 percent of facility energy or water use at each agency.

children.label: i
children.text: the use of a system to manage energy and water use at the facility; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the applicability of the certification of the facility in accordance with the International Organization for Standardization standard numbered 50001 and entitled “Energy Management Systems”.

children.heading: Energy and water evaluations and commissioning
children.label: 3 Energy and water evaluations and commissioning

children.heading: Evaluations
children.label: A Evaluations
children.text: Except as provided in subparagraph (B), not later than the date that is 180 days after , and annually thereafter, each energy manager shall complete, for the preceding calendar year, a comprehensive energy and water evaluation and recommissioning or retrocommissioning for approximately 25 percent of the facilities of the applicable agency that meet the criteria under paragraph (2)(B) in a manner that ensures that an evaluation of each facility is completed not less frequently than once every 4 years.December 27, 2020

children.label: i
children.text: has had a comprehensive energy and water evaluation during the preceding 8-year period;

children.label: I
children.text: has been commissioned, recommissioned, or retrocommissioned during the preceding 10-year period; or

children.label: II
children.text: is under ongoing commissioning, recommissioning, or retrocomissioning;

children.label: iii
children.text: has not had a major change in function or use since the previous evaluation and recommissioning or retrocommissioning;

children.label: iv
children.text: has been benchmarked with public disclosure under paragraph (8) during the preceding calendar year; and

children.label: aa
children.text: the date of the most recent evaluation; or

children.label: AA
children.text: of the most recent commissioning, recommissioning, or retrocommissioning; or

children.label: BB
children.text: on which ongoing commissioning began; or

children.label: II
children.text: has a long-term contract in place guaranteeing energy savings at least as great as the energy savings target under subclause (I).

children.heading: Implementation of identified energy and water efficiency measures
children.label: 4 Implementation of identified energy and water efficiency measures
i
children.text: the Federal agency identified in the evaluation; and

children.label: ii
children.text: is life cycle cost-effective, as determined by evaluating an individual measure or a bundle of measures with varying paybacks.

children.heading: Performance contracting
children.label: B Performance contracting
children.text: Each Federal agency shall use performance contracting to address at least 50 percent of the measures identified under subparagraph (A)(i).

children.label: A
children.text: equipment, including building and equipment controls, is fully commissioned at acceptance to be operating at design specifications;

children.label: B
children.text: a plan for appropriate operations, maintenance, and repair of the equipment is in place at acceptance and is followed;

children.label: C
children.text: equipment and system performance is measured during its entire life to ensure proper operations, maintenance, and repair; and

children.label: D
children.text: energy and water savings are measured and verified.

children.heading: Relationship to funding source
children.label: B Relationship to funding source
children.text: The guidelines issued by the Secretary under subparagraph (A) shall be appropriate and uniform for measures funded with each type of funding made available under paragraph (10), but may distinguish between different types of measures project size, and other criteria the Secretary determines are relevant.1So in original. A comma probably should appear.1

children.text: to publish energy and water consumption data on an individual facility basis.

children.label: II

children.label: III

children.label: IV

children.label: V

children.label: I
children.text: can be accomplished with the use of streamlined procedures and templates that minimize the time demands on Federal employees; and

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Subject to clause (ii), the Secretary shall make the web-based tracking system required under this paragraph available to Congress, other Federal agencies, and the public through the Internet.

children.heading: Exemptions
children.label: ii Exemptions
children.text: At the request of a Federal agency, the Secretary may exempt specific data for specific facilities from disclosure under clause (i) for national security purposes.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The energy manager shall enter energy use data for each metered building that is (or is a part of) a facility that meets the criteria established by the Secretary under paragraph (2)(B) into a building energy use benchmarking system, such as the Energy Star Portfolio Manager.

children.label: i
children.text: select or develop the building energy use benchmarking system required under this paragraph for each type of building; and

children.label: ii
children.text: issue guidance for use of the system.

children.heading: Public disclosure
children.label: C Public disclosure
children.text: Each energy manager shall post the information entered into, or generated by, a benchmarking system under this subsection, on the web-based tracking system under paragraph (7)(B). The energy manager shall update such information each year, and shall include in such reporting previous years’ information to allow changes in building performance to be tracked over time.

children.label: i
children.text: summaries of the status of implementing the various requirements of the agency and its energy managers under this subsection; and

children.label: ii
children.text: any other means of measuring performance that the Director considers appropriate.

children.heading: Availability
children.label: B Availability
children.text: The Director shall make the scorecards required under this paragraph available to Congress, other Federal agencies, and the public through the Internet.

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: A Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There are authorized to be appropriated such sums as are necessary to carry out this subsection.

children.label: I
children.text: appropriated funds made available under subparagraph (A); and

children.label: II
children.text: private financing otherwise authorized under Federal law, including financing available through energy savings performance contracts or utility energy service contracts.

children.heading: Combined funding for same measure
children.label: ii Combined funding for same measure
children.text: A Federal agency may use any combination of appropriated funds and private financing described in clause (i) to carry out the same measure under this subsection.

children.heading: Implementation
children.label: C Implementation
children.text: Each Federal agency may implement the requirements under this subsection itself or may contract out performance of some or all of the requirements.

children.heading: Rule of construction
children.label: 11 Rule of construction
children.text: This subsection shall not be construed to require or to obviate any contractor savings guarantees.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Each Federal agency shall ensure that any large capital energy investment in an existing building that is not a major renovation but involves replacement of installed equipment (such as heating and cooling systems), or involves renovation, rehabilitation, expansion, or remodeling of existing space, employs the most energy efficient designs, systems, equipment, and controls that are life-cycle cost effective.

children.heading: Process for review of investment decisions
children.label: 2 Process for review of investment decisions
A
children.text: develop a process for reviewing each decision made on a large capital energy investment described in paragraph (1) to ensure that the requirements of this subsection are met; and

children.label: B
children.text: report to the Director of the Office of Management and Budget on the process established.

children.heading: Compliance report
children.label: 3 Compliance report
children.text: Not later than 1 year after , the Director of the Office of Management and Budget shall evaluate and report to Congress on the compliance of each agency with this subsection.December 19, 2007

children.heading: Federal implementation strategy for energy-efficient and energy-saving information technologies
children.label: h Federal implementation strategy for energy-efficient and energy-saving information technologies

children.heading: Director
children.label: A Director
children.text: The term “Director” means the Director of the Office of Management and Budget.

children.heading: Information technology
children.label: B Information technology
children.text: The term “information technology” has the meaning given that term in .section 11101 of title 40

children.heading: Development of implementation strategy
children.label: 2 Development of implementation strategy
children.text: Not later than 1 year after , each Federal agency shall coordinate with the Director, the Secretary, and the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency to develop an implementation strategy (including best-practices and measurement and verification techniques) for the maintenance, purchase, and use by the Federal agency of energy-efficient and energy-saving information technologies at or for facilities owned and operated by the Federal agency, taking into consideration the performance goals established under paragraph (4).December 27, 2020

children.label: A
children.text: advanced metering infrastructure;

children.label: B
children.text: energy efficient data center strategies and methods of increasing asset and infrastructure utilization;

children.label: C
children.text: advanced power management tools;

children.label: D
children.text: building information modeling, including building energy management;

children.label: E
children.text: secure telework and travel substitution tools; and

children.label: F
children.text: mechanisms to ensure that the agency realizes the energy cost savings of increased efficiency and utilization.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Not later than 180 days after , the Director, in consultation with the Secretary, shall establish performance goals for evaluating the efforts of Federal agencies in improving the maintenance, purchase, and use of energy-efficient and energy-saving information technology at or for facilities owned and operated by the Federal agencies.December 27, 2020

children.label: i
children.text: energy savings performance contracting; and

children.label: ii
children.text: utility energy services contracting.

children.heading: Agency reports
children.label: A Agency reports
children.text: Each Federal agency shall include in the report of the agency under a description of the efforts and results of the agency under this subsection.section 17143 of this title

children.heading: OMB government efficiency reports and scorecards
children.label: B OMB government efficiency reports and scorecards
children.text: Effective beginning not later than , the Director shall include in the annual report and scorecard of the Director required under a description of the efforts and results of Federal agencies under this subsection.October 1, 2022section 17144 of this title

children.heading: Use of existing reporting structures
children.label: C Use of existing reporting structures
children.text: The Director may require Federal agencies to submit any information required to be submitted under this subsection though reporting structures in use as of .December 27, 2020

children.label: A
children.text: to coordinate and strengthen Federal energy and water resilience;

children.label: B
children.text: to promote environmental stewardship; and

children.label: C
children.text: to reduce energy consumption during periods of unusually high electricity or natural gas demand.

children.heading: Federal Director
children.label: 2 Federal Director
children.text: The Secretary shall appoint an individual to serve as the director of the Program (referred to in this subsection as the “Federal Director”), which shall be a career position in the Senior Executive service, to administer the Program.

children.heading: Strategic planning and technical assistance
children.label: A Strategic planning and technical assistance
i
children.text: provide technical assistance and project implementation support and guidance to agencies to identify, implement, procure, and track energy and water conservation measures required under this chapter and under other provisions of law;

children.label: ii

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: 5 Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There is authorized to be appropriated to the Secretary to carry out this subsection $36,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2021 through 2025.
